title: Chip Selloff Rocks Tech Stocks | Open Interest 7/13/2026
title_cn: 芯片抛售震撼科技股|未平仓合约 7/13/2026
summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Markets hit another tech speed bump as SK Hynix sparks a chip selloff, while Wall Street braces for bank earnings and critical inflation data. Oil jumps after fresh strikes and President Trump vows to take over the Strait of Hormuz. Plus, exclusive interviews with Sunrun CEO Mary Powell on turning homes into AI hubs, General Fusion CEO Greg Twinney after the company's Nasdaq debut, KBW's Chris McGratty on the biggest bank earnings risks, and Lazard's George Bilicic on powering the AI boom. (Source: Bloomberg)

(Source: Bloomberg)

summary: An American aid worker who tested positive for Ebola is stable and being treated in a specialized isolation unit at Frankfurt University Hospital in Germany, according to a statement from the hospital.

title: Wall Street Banks Set to Pull in Almost $39 Billion From Trading
title_cn: 华尔街银行将从交易中吸纳近 390 亿美元
summary:
The biggest US banks — set to kick off a marathon Tuesday with five of those firms reporting second-quarter earnings — are reaping the benefits of a volatile past few months that’s spurred more action from clients wanting to trade.
Chris McGratty, Head of US Bank Research at KBW, previews what to expect from bank earnings. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Sunrun Offers Solar Users Hundreds Per Month in Compute Program
title_cn: Sunrun 为太阳能用户提供每月数百美元的计算计划
summary: Sunrun Inc. is piloting a program aimed at enabling customers to earn hundreds of dollars per month that would use electricity from rooftop solar and battery installations to power home-computing systems for AI, according to Chief Executive Officer Mary Powell.

title: SK Hynix ADRs Tumble in Second Trading Day After Korea Selloff
title_cn: 韩国抛售后，SK 海力士 ADR 在第二个交易日暴跌
summary:
An AI-fueled stock rout in South Korea spilled over into the US market Monday, as SK Hynix Inc. American depositary shares fell as much as 9.3%, underscoring growing investor concerns that the boom is overextended.

The decline brought ADRs to trade just a few dollars above the $149 level where they priced last week ahead of their trading debut. Memory and storage peers in US dropped across the board, with Micron Technology Inc., Sandisk Corp., and Western Digital Corp. shares all falling more than 6%. The slump came after shares of SK Hynix plunged a record 15% in South Korea, sending the benchmark Kospi index down 9% and triggering a market-wide trading suspension.

For more, we speak with Mandeep Singh, Global Head of Tech Research for Bloomberg Intelligence. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Carr Accuses Disney of Being 'Disingenuous' of FCC Inquiries
title_cn: 卡尔指责迪士尼在 FCC 调查中“不诚实”
summary: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr calls Walt Disney Co. "disingenuous" regarding the probe into the company's diversity and inclusion practices. He speaks on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: FCC Cut Satellite Approval Backlog in Half, Carr Says
title_cn: 卡尔表示，FCC 将积压的卫星审批数量减少了一半
summary: The Federal Communications Commission has reduced the backlog of satellite application approvals by half as it broadly seeks to stimulate the space and communications economy, Chairman Brendan Carr said in an interview on Bloomberg TV.

title: More Than 200 Experts Urge Action to Steer AI for Society’s Good
title_cn: 200 多位专家敦促采取行动引导人工智能造福社会
summary: A group of top economists and artificial intelligence researchers called for more study of the technology’s likely impact, saying it may become “radically more powerful” over the next decade and needs to be steered in a human-friendly direction.

title: FCC's Carr on Approval Times, Paramount-Warner Deal and Disney
title_cn: FCC 的卡尔谈审批时间、派拉蒙-华纳交易和迪士尼
summary: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr discusses the need to speed up approval times for spectrum and orbital data centers. He also talks about Paramount Skydance trying to buy Warner Bros. Discovery and the importance of broadcasters acting in the public interest. He speaks on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Lazard's Bilicic Sees 'Tremendous' Demand for AI Data Center Power
title_cn: Lazard 的 Bilicic 看到人工智能数据中心电力的“巨大”需求
summary: George Bilicic, Lazard's global head of power, energy and infrastructure, says we're a long way from solving the issue of energy demand for AI data center power and expects upward pressure on gas prices. He speaks on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Sunrun's Experiment to Turn Homes Into AI Hubs
title_cn: Sunrun 将家庭变成人工智能中心的实验
summary: Sunrun CEO Mary Powell joins Open Interest to explain how the company plans to transform homes with solar and batteries into AI computing hubs. She says homeowners could earn hundreds of dollars a month by hosting AI compute nodes while helping meet soaring demand for AI infrastructure. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: General Fusion Soars After Nasdaq Debut
title_cn: General Fusion 纳斯达克上市后股价飙升
summary: General Fusion CEO Greg Twinney joins Open Interest after the company's Nasdaq debut, explaining why going public accelerates its fusion roadmap, how fusion could power AI data centers, and why he believes the company's mechanical approach can outperform rival technologies in the global race for commercial fusion energy. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Gas-Power Costs at 17-Year High and Climbing, Lazard Says
title_cn: Lazard 称美国天然气发电成本创 17 年来新高且仍在攀升
summary: The cost of power from natural gas-fired plants in the US hit the highest level in at least 17 years, and is poised to climb even higher as demand surges from new data centers.

title: AI Chip Giants TSMC and SK Hynix Pull in Different Directions
title_cn: AI芯片巨头台积电和SK海力士分道扬镳
summary:
TSMC soared while SK Hynix tumbled — why are are the two essential AI chip firms pulling in different directions?

Tom Mackenzie explains. (Source: Bloomberg)

summary: Investors are flocking back to Apple Inc. as nervousness about artificial intelligence spending weighs on the stocks of chipmakers and cloud-computing giants.

title: TSMC’s Sales Soar 36% in Latest Sign of AI Spending Momentum
title_cn: 人工智能支出势头最新迹象，台积电销售额飙升 36%
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. reported a 36% jump in quarterly sales, meeting elevated expectations while signaling global demand for AI computing remains intact.

title: Xi to Debut at China’s Flagship AI Summit as US Rivalry Heats Up
title_cn: 习近平将在中国人工智能旗舰峰会上首次亮相，美国竞争加剧
summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping will make his first appearance at the country’s flagship AI conference, signaling the growing importance Beijing attaches to the technology as its rivalry with the US intensifies.

title: Saudi Arabia Becomes Top Buyer in Taiwan’s Young Drone Industry
title_cn: 沙特阿拉伯成为台湾年轻无人机产业的最大买家
summary: Saudi Arabia bought a record $47.2 million of drones from Taiwan last month — a sign the island’s nascent UAV industry is becoming more attractive to buyers.

title: Why AI Might Actually Create More Work for Lawyers
title_cn: 为什么人工智能实际上可能为律师创造更多工作
summary: It seems obvious that among the many industries that AI might disrupt, the legal profession might face some of the most adverse outcomes. When clerical, research-based tasks like searching through databases and reading contracts are automated, what is left for lawyers to do and how might they justify all those billable hours? In this episode we speak with Gary Wingens, chair and partner at the law firm Lowenstein Sandler. He talks about how his firm is using AI and why he thinks the technology could end up increasing legal work for lawyers as costs come down, creating a sort of “Jevon's paradox” for lawsuits, deals and litigation. We also talk about the billable hours model and training junior talent. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Morgan Stanley’s Wilson Sees Profit Boost for Stocks Beyond Tech
title_cn: 摩根士丹利的威尔逊认为科技股以外的股票利润将得到提振
summary: US stocks beyond the technology giants are poised to report strong earnings this season, broadening the equity rally, according to Morgan Stanley strategists.

title: South Korea to Tap Record AI Tax Windfall to Fund Future Growth
title_cn: 韩国将利用创纪录的人工智能税收暴利为未来增长提供资金
summary: South Korea expects to reap a record tax windfall from the artificial intelligence-driven semiconductor boom, giving President Lee Jae Myung’s government fresh fiscal firepower to fund an ambitious investment agenda.

title: Judge Rules Trump IRS Immunity Deal Has No ‘Basis in Law’
title_cn: 法官裁定特朗普国税局豁免协议没有“法律依据”
summary: A federal judge ruled that President Donald Trump’s lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service was a “bad faith” attempt to manipulate the judicial process and barred him and his administration from citing its supposed settlement in any future regulatory or judicial proceedings.

title: Venezuela’s Inflation Spike Compounds Earthquake Misery
title_cn: 委内瑞拉通胀飙升加剧了地震带来的痛苦
summary: Venezuela’s monthly inflation more than doubled in June, deepening the hardship for families already reeling from the devastation caused by the twin earthquakes of June 24.

title: ICE Fatally Shoots Man in Maine as Agency Scrutiny Intensifies
title_cn: 随着机构审查的加强，ICE 在缅因州射杀一名男子
summary: A US immigration officer shot and killed a person in Maine on Monday morning, the second fatal shooting involving federal agents in less than a week.

title: Trump’s Temporary Jones Act Waiver Sparks Long-Term Policy Fight
title_cn: 特朗普的《琼斯法案》临时豁免引发长期政策斗争
summary: Energy and shipping industry officials are locked in an increasingly bitter lobbying fight over the future of the century-old Jones Act, battling to determine the long-term fate of the critical protectionist law.

title: Mexico Pushes Criminal Probe Into Migrant Deaths Linked to ICE
title_cn: 墨西哥对与 ICE 有关的移民死亡事件展开刑事调查
summary: President Claudia Sheinbaum defended her government’s push to get US prosecutors to investigate the mounting death toll of Mexican nationals detained by immigration agents while shrugging off the possibility her advocacy might anger Donald Trump.

title: EU Sanctions Sudan’s Gold Trade as Civil War Rages
title_cn: 内战愈演愈烈，欧盟制裁苏丹黄金贸易
summary: The European Union approved new trade prohibitions on Sudan’s gold sector as the country’s civil war enters its fourth year.

title: EU Fails to Adopt Russian Sanctions and Freeze Oil Price Cap
title_cn: 欧盟未能通过俄罗斯制裁并冻结石油价格上限
summary: The European Union failed to endorse a 21st sanctions package against Russia on Monday, putting the bloc at risk of undermining one of its key tools to restrict the Kremlin’s oil revenue.

title: Hungary Passes Law to Oust President in Orban-Era Break
title_cn: 匈牙利通过法律推翻欧尔班时代总统
summary: Hungarian lawmakers approved a constitutional amendment ousting the Viktor Orban-allied president, delivering on Prime Minister Peter Magyar’s pledge for a clean break from his nationalist predecessor. The forint dropped.

title: FCC's Carr Says 'Hard to See' Antitrust Paramount Suit
title_cn: FCC 的卡尔表示“很难看到”反垄断派拉蒙诉讼
summary: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr says it's "hard to see" a legitimate antitrust action against the Paramount Skydance purchase of Warner Bros. Discovery. He speaks on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ICE Renews Bid to Transform NJ Warehouse Into Detention Facility
title_cn: ICE 再次竞标将新泽西州仓库改造成拘留设施
summary: The Trump administration is reconsidering plans to convert a New Jersey warehouse into an immigration detention facility, despite previously telling a federal court that the agency abandoned such efforts.

title: UK, EU Reach Deal on British Access to €60 Billion Ukraine Loan
title_cn: 英国与欧盟就英国获得 600 亿欧元乌克兰贷款达成协议
summary: UK companies will be able to win contracts funded through a €60 billion ($68 billion) defense procurement loan from the European Union to Ukraine.

title: Trump Recommends Graham’s Sister Serve Out His Senate Term
title_cn: 特朗普建议格雷厄姆的妹妹完成参议员任期
summary: President Donald Trump said the late Senator Lindsey Graham’s sister, Darline Graham Nordone, should serve out his term for the South Carolina seat.

title: UK Counter-Terror Police Lead Probe Into Murder of Ann Widdecombe
title_cn: 英国反恐警察牵头调查安·维德科姆谋杀案
summary: UK counter-terrorism police said they have taken over the investigation into the murder of Reform politician Ann Widdecombe after new evidence emerged about her death last week.

title: US Expands Cuba Sanctions With Targeting of Tourism Industry
title_cn: 美国扩大对古巴制裁，针对旅游业
summary: The US added Cuba’s Ministry of Tourism to its expanding sanctions list, striking once-again at one of the main economic drivers of the communist-run Caribbean island.

title: European Powers Join Forces to Build Anti-Ballistic Systems
title_cn: 欧洲强国联手建立反弹道系统
summary: A coalition of European nations agreed to share research and industrial experience to build an anti-ballistic missile defense, moving away from its reliance on the US as Ukraine struggles against Russia’s onslaught.

title: Regulators Unveil Guidance Targeting Bank Services to Immigrants
title_cn: 监管机构公布针对移民的银行服务指南
summary: Top US bank agencies unveiled guidance Monday that calls on lenders to manage the way they engage immigrant clients who may present “elevated” risks.

title: Crimea Loses Fuel, Water and Power, But Not Russian Tourists
title_cn: 克里米亚失去了燃料、水和电力，但俄罗斯游客却没有
summary: Russian tourists are continuing to take their summer holidays in Crimea despite persistent Ukrainian drone attacks, a state of emergency, fuel shortages and power outages on the annexed Black Sea peninsula.

title: Putin Puts Akzo Nobel’s Local Assets Under Temporary Control
title_cn: 普京将阿克苏诺贝尔的当地资产置于临时控制之下
summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin ordered stakes in three Russian companies owned by Dutch paints and coatings maker Akzo Nobel NV transferred to temporary administration.

title: Pentagon Spending on Mexican Border Operations Tops $2.6 Billion
title_cn: 五角大楼在墨西哥边境行动上的支出突破 26 亿美元
summary: The Pentagon has spent at least $2.64 billion on operations at the southwest border with Mexico, according to Congress’s audit agency in the most comprehensive cost accounting yet of the military’s expanded role in immigration enforcement.

title: Switzerland and UK Agree Trade Deal to Deepen Post-Brexit Ties
title_cn: 瑞士和英国达成贸易协议以深化脱欧后关系
summary: Switzerland and the UK concluded talks on an updated free-trade agreement, bringing Western Europe’s two major non-European Union economies closer together with a pact focused on services.

title: Merz Urges Currency Dialogue with China as Trade Worries Rise
title_cn: 随着贸易担忧加剧，默茨敦促与中国进行货币对话
summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz reinforced a call for dialogue with China to address concerns that the world’s second-largest economy is shoring up an unfair trade advantage by keeping its currency artificially low.

title: Brazil Draft Rule Opens Uranium Mining to Private Partners
title_cn: 巴西规则草案向私人合作伙伴开放铀矿开采
summary: Brazil plans to open its uranium mining sector to private investment by allowing partnerships with companies, provided the state-owned nuclear company retains at least a 20% stake in each venture, according to a draft regulation seen by Bloomberg.

title: US and Iran Trade Strikes, Dispute Whether Hormuz Is Open
title_cn: 美国和伊朗贸易打击，霍尔木兹海峡是否开放存在争议
summary: The US and Iran exchanged fresh strikes overnight into Monday, prolonging a spate of tit-for-tat attacks at a time when the two nations offer conflicting statements over whether the Strait of Hormuz is open to shipping.

title: DOJ Is Probing Claims UAW’s President Abused Union Position
title_cn: 司法部正在调查 UAW 主席滥用工会职位的指控
summary: The US Justice Department launched a grand jury investigation into allegations that the head of the United Auto Workers pressured another high-ranking union official to secure benefits for his fiancée and her sister, according to internal communications within the labor organization.

title: How the Sudden Death of Sen. Graham Will Shift the GOP
title_cn: 参议员格雷厄姆的突然去世将如何改变共和党
summary: Jon Lieber of the Eurasia Group reflects on how the sudden death of Senator Lindsey Graham of South Carolina will impact President Donald Trump and foreign policy in the Republican party. He speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." Graham died on Saturday at age 71. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: California Lawmakers Push Fix for Hollywood Tax Credits
title_cn: 加州立法者推动修复好莱坞税收抵免
summary: A California business tax credit cap aimed at saving $4 billion threatens to drive away film and television production, according to a letter signed by a bipartisan group of lawmakers who demanded a quick fix to the budget signed by Governor Gavin Newsom.
